sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SPS Commerce (SPSC) Q4 Earnings Beat Estimates
feed.summary: SPS Commerce (SPSC) reported Q4 earnings of $1.14 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1 per share and exceeding last year's $0.89. Despite the earnings beat, the company's Q4 revenues of $192.65 million missed the consensus estimate by 0.45%. SPS Commerce shares have lost 23.1% year-to-date, and the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: |【即時新聞】YieldMax ABNB選擇權收益策略ETF宣布每股0.2213美元週配息，除息日程一次掌握
feed.summary: |YieldMax ABNB選擇權收益策略ETF (ABNY) 宣布將配發每股0.2213美元的週配息，除息日和股東名冊基準日均設定為2月5日，股利發放日為2月6日。此公告為關注該ETF短期收益的投資人提供了關鍵數據，並詳述了ABNY的最新收盤行情和交易量變化。

feed.title: SCE Extends More Than 500 Offers for Eaton Fire Recovery in Just Over 100 Days
feed.summary: Southern California Edison (SCE) has reached a major milestone in its Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program, extending over 500 compensation offers totaling more than $165 million to individuals and businesses affected by the Eaton Fire. The program aims to provide streamlined and faster compensation than traditional litigation. SCE encourages claimants to gather documents, check emails for updates, and respond promptly to information requests to accelerate the process.

feed.title: Cencora raises $3B to repay 364-day credit for OneOncology acquisition
feed.summary: Cencora, Inc. has successfully closed a $3.0 billion senior notes offering, consisting of five tranches with varying interest rates and maturity dates ranging from 2029 to 2056. The net proceeds from this offering will primarily be used to repay its 364-Day Term Credit Agreement, which was obtained to finance a portion of its acquisition of OneOncology, with any remaining funds allocated for general corporate purposes. The offering was an underwritten registered public offering managed by several major financial institutions.

feed.title: W. R. Berkley Corporation Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of 9 cents per share on its common stock. The dividend will be paid on March 4, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 23, 2026. The company is an insurance holding company and a major commercial lines writer in the U.S.

feed.title: Insurer W. R. Berkley to pay 9¢ a share dividend on Mar. 4
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation (NYSE: WRB) has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of 9 cents per share. This dividend will be paid on March 4, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 23, 2026. The company, founded in 1967, is a major commercial lines writer in the United States.

feed.title: Western Digital execs to speak at Morgan Stanley investor event
feed.summary: Western Digital Corporation announced that its management will participate in the upcoming Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. A live webcast of the presentation will be available on WD’s Investor Relations website, with an archived replay accessible afterward. Western Digital aims to highlight its role in the AI-driven data economy, providing storage infrastructure and innovative platforms.

feed.title: Leidos to pay shareholders $0.43 per share on March 31, 2026
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings, Inc. has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share, payable on March 31, 2026. Shareholders of record by the close of business on March 16, 2026, will be eligible to receive this dividend. The announcement was made on February 13, 2026, by the company's board of directors.

feed.title: Humana unit buys Florida clinic network serving 120,000 patients
feed.summary: Humana's CenterWell healthcare services division has completed its acquisition of MaxHealth, a Florida-based primary care organization. The acquisition expands CenterWell Senior Primary Care's reach with MaxHealth's network of 82 clinics serving over 120,000 patients, including 80,000 in value-based care programs. This move aims to provide personalized and integrated care to more patients in new key markets.

feed.title: Honeywell to pay $1.19-a-share dividend in March payout
feed.summary: Honeywell (NASDAQ: HON) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share, declared by its Board of Directors on February 13, 2026. This dividend is payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on February 27, 2026, and will be paid out of surplus. The company also provided details on its operations as an integrated operating company serving various industries globally.

feed.title: Travere Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TVTX) Stock Price Down 7.9% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Travere Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TVTX) saw its stock price drop by 7.9% on Friday, trading below its 50-day simple moving average. Despite this decline, analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $38.62. Insider selling has occurred with approximately 4.19% ownership remaining, while institutional investors have adjusted their positions.

feed.title: NVIDIA vs. SMCI: Which AI Hardware Stock Is the Better Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article compares NVIDIA (NVDA) and Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI) as AI hardware stock investments. While both companies benefit from the AI infrastructure boom, NVIDIA is presented as the stronger buy due to its robust profitability, higher gross margins, and lower financial risk compared to Supermicro, which faces margin pressure despite strong revenue growth. NVIDIA holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), while Supermicro has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Pool Corporation Announces Director Departure
feed.summary: Pool Corporation (POOL) announced that director Carlos A. Sabater will not seek re-election at the company's 2026 annual meeting of shareholders,

feed.title: Danaher Board Change As Jessica Mega Retires And Governance Focus Grows
feed.summary: Danaher Corporation announced the retirement of Dr. Jessica L. Mega from its Board of Directors, effective February 5, 2026, due to other professional commitments. This change is considered a material governance event, though Danaher clarified there are no disagreements. Investors are advised to monitor the board's future appointments and how Danaher navigates sector pressures like constrained biotech funding and pricing challenges in China.

feed.title: Does Weyerhaeuser’s (WY) Cash Return Framework Reveal Its True Capital Allocation Priorities?
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (WY) recently declared a quarterly base dividend and reaffirmed its cash return framework, aiming to distribute 75-80% of annual adjusted funds through base dividends, supplemental payouts, and opportunistic share repurchases. This strategy links shareholder returns directly to the company's Timberlands and Wood Products cash flows, highlighting the importance of housing demand and lumber pricing for its financial performance. While offering potential upside, it also exposes investors to risks from weaker lumber prices or a slowdown in the housing market.

feed.title: BNY Deepens Cross Border Role With Chilwa ADR And Williams F1 Deal
feed.summary: BNY Mellon has expanded its footprint by being appointed as the depositary bank for Chilwa Minerals' new ADR program, aiming for a Nasdaq listing, and forming a long-term partnership with the Williams F1 Team to boost branding and client engagement. These strategic moves highlight BNY's efforts to support issuers entering US capital markets and broaden its brand reach, following a period of significant stock price growth. While the ADR program is expected to generate fees and deepen issuer relationships, the F1 sponsorship aims to enhance brand visibility, with investors advised to monitor future disclosures for measurable impacts on earnings and valuation.

feed.title: NextEra Energy Transmission and Exelon Welcome PJM Board Vote to Advance Grid Reliability Project
feed.summary: NextEra Energy Transmission and Exelon Corporation announced that the PJM Board of Directors has approved their proposed 220-mile, 765-kV transmission line project. This initiative aims to bolster electric reliability and affordability for West Virginia, Pennsylvania, and the MidAtlantic region by adding critical capacity, enabling new generation, and attracting economic development. The companies emphasize their commitment to community engagement and minimizing land impacts as the project progresses.

feed.title: PJM backs 220-mile power line to keep electricity affordable in WV and PA
feed.summary: The PJM Board has approved a proposed 220-mile, 765-kV transmission line by NextEra Energy Transmission and Exelon, aimed at enhancing grid reliability and affordability in West Virginia, Pennsylvania, and the Mid-Atlantic region. This project is expected to support new generation connections, create jobs, and stimulate economic development, addressing rising electricity demand. Both companies emphasize the line's importance for long-term power stability and customer affordability.

feed.title: Henschen, Marathon Petroleum ex VP, sells $1.07 million in shares
feed.summary: Michael A. Henschen II, former Executive Vice President of Refining at Marathon Petroleum Corp, sold 5,289 shares of company stock for approximately $1.07 million. This transaction occurred on February 12, 2026, and follows strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings reported by Marathon Petroleum, which exceeded analyst expectations. Analysts have consequently raised their price targets for the company.

feed.title: Jacobs Solutions, Planet Labs, NetApp, TaskUs, and Viasat Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Jacobs Solutions, Planet Labs, NetApp, TaskUs, and Viasat, experienced a jump in value following a softer-than-anticipated Consumer Price Index (CPI) report. This fueled investor optimism for potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, leading to a rally in both stocks and Treasuries. The article details the specific percentage gains for each company and provides further context on NetApp's recent performance.

feed.title: Caterpillar group president De Lange sells $9.59m in stock
feed.summary: Caterpillar Group President Bob De Lange sold 12,507 shares of common stock for approximately $9.59 million on February 11, 2026. He also exercised options to acquire 15,000 shares and then disposed of 2,493 shares. Following these transactions, De Lange directly owns 84,997 shares of Caterpillar, and this news follows the company's strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report.

feed.title: Quality is key: Investing in advanced nuclear research for tomorrow’s grid
feed.summary: This article highlights Southern Company's crucial role in advancing nuclear technology to meet future energy demands for reliability and affordability. It emphasizes the company's hands-on innovation in next-generation nuclear systems rather than just being a customer. The article suggests that continued investment in advanced nuclear research is key for the energy sector.

feed.title: How to watch Amgen at Citi's 2026 virtual oncology summit
feed.summary: Amgen (NASDAQ: AMGN) is scheduled to present at Citi's 2026 Virtual Oncology Leadership Summit on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at 12:15 p.m. PT. Jean-Charles Soria, SVP of oncology within global development, will deliver the presentation. The event will be webcast live and archived for at least 90 days on Amgen's investor website, with details available on their Investor Relations Events Calendar.

feed.title: TUESDAY DEADLINE: F5, Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action, Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP Announces
feed.summary: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP announced a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) for alleged violations of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. Investors who suffered substantial losses are encouraged to seek lead plaintiff status by the deadline of Tuesday, February 17, 2026. The lawsuit claims F5 misrepresented its revenue outlook and minimized security risks, particularly after disclosing a significant nation-state security breach affecting its BIG-IP product, leading to stock price drops.

feed.title: Albertsons, Burlington, Target, and Williams-Sonoma Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Albertsons, Burlington, Target, and Williams-Sonoma jumped following a softer-than-expected January Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, which saw inflation rise by only 0.2%. This data has increased market expectations for the Federal Reserve to begin cutting interest rates as early as June, positively impacting stock valuations. Burlington, in particular, has shown volatility but recently reported strong Q4 2024 results with significant comparable store sales growth, leading to an optimistic outlook despite economic uncertainties.

feed.title: What 6 Analyst Ratings Have To Say About Ameren
feed.summary: Six analysts have recently provided ratings for Ameren (NYSE: AEE), revealing a mix of bullish and indifferent sentiments. The average 12-month price target is $112.33, showing a slight decrease from the previous average. Ameren, a utility company, demonstrates strong financial performance with positive revenue growth, high net margin, and efficient asset utilization, despite a slightly higher debt-to-equity ratio than its peers.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for Automatic Data Processing
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing Inc. (NASDAQ: ADP) is currently trading at $211.55, reflecting a 0.76% increase in the current market session, despite a significant decline of 18.77% over the past month and 31.75% over the past year. The company's P/E ratio of 20.17 is higher than the Professional Services industry's aggregate P/E of 18.14, suggesting potential outperformance or possible overvaluation. Investors are cautioned to use the P/E ratio as part of a comprehensive analysis, considering other financial metrics, industry trends, and qualitative factors, as a low P/E could also indicate weak growth or financial instability.

feed.title: Lau Michele, McKesson EVP, sells $282k in MCK stock
feed.summary: Michele Lau, Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer at McKesson CORP (NYSE:MCK), sold 303 shares of common stock for $282,817 on February 11, 2026. This transaction was executed under a Rule 10b5-1(c) plan. Following the sale, Lau directly holds 5,972 shares and indirectly owns additional shares through a retirement plan.

feed.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill to open at Sheffield Crossing in Sand Springs, city says
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill is set to open a new location in Sand Springs, Oklahoma, within the Sheffield Crossing development along Highway 97. Construction is anticipated to conclude by the fourth quarter of 2026, creating new jobs and boosting the local economy. City officials express confidence that Chipotle's investment signifies the city's growth and aligns with its economic development goals.

feed.title: Utility Stocks Ameren, CenterPoint Power Up In Buy Zones Amid Volatile Stock Market
feed.summary: Utility stocks Ameren (AEE) and CenterPoint Energy (CNP) have reached new highs despite recent stock market volatility, which was partly driven by fears surrounding artificial intelligence. Both companies are featured on Investor's Business Daily's New Highs list. This strong performance occurred as the tech-heavy Nasdaq composite and S&P 500 experienced solid losses, indicating a potential flight to defensive sectors like utilities during uncertain times.

feed.title: Angle Advisors announces Brunner Manufacturing has been acquired by Howmet Aerospace
feed.summary: Angle Advisors announced that Brunner Manufacturing has been acquired by Howmet Aerospace, becoming part of Howmet Fastening Systems. Brunner, a leading manufacturer of fasteners and high-strength components for agricultural, industrial, and commercial vehicles, will allow Howmet to expand its product portfolio and manufacturing capabilities. Angle Advisors acted as the exclusive investment banking advisor to Brunner in this transaction.

feed.title: Taseko to Release Fourth Quarter and Year End 2025 Results
feed.summary: Taseko Mines Limited (TSX: TKO; NYSE American: TGB; LSE: TKO) announced it will release its fourth quarter and year-end 2025 financial results after market close on Wednesday, February 18, 2026. The company will host a telephone conference call and live webcast on Thursday, February 19, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results, which will include a question and answer session for analysts and investors.

feed.title: Generac Stock Rallies: Why AI Matters More Than Earnings
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. (GNRC) shares surged by 18% despite missing Q4 earnings estimates, driven by strong 2026 guidance and a strategic pivot towards providing backup power for AI data centers. Investors overlooked the Q4 misses, which were attributed to temporary weather patterns and a one-time product liability settlement, focusing instead on the significant growth projected for the Commercial & Industrial segment due to AI-driven demand and a new share repurchase program. The market views Generac as transforming from a "weather stock" to a critical infrastructure provider for the AI revolution.

feed.title: TER Teradyne, Inc. (NASDAQ) $314.05 intraday 13 Feb 2026: AI demand guides outlook
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) experienced a 2.30% intraday decline to $314.05 on February 13, 2026, amid profit-taking after a rally and sector rotation towards AI chip demand. Despite this, the stock shows strong long-term appreciation with its exposure to AI hardware cycles through its Semiconductor Test and Industrial Automation segments. Meyka AI rates TER as a "BUY" with a B+ grade (76.51/100), balancing its high valuation against strong cash generation and low leverage, though its forecasting model projects short-term downside.

feed.title: J&J’s baby powder caused a woman’s fatal cancer, Philly jury finds, awarding her family $250,000
feed.summary: A Philadelphia jury has ordered Johnson & Johnson to pay $250,000 to the family of Gayle Emerson, finding that the company's talc-based baby powder caused her ovarian cancer. Emerson, who died months after filing her complaint in 2019, was part of a nationwide wave of litigation against J&J. The verdict included $50,000 in compensatory damages and $200,000 in punitive damages, despite J&J's defense that the baby powder was not responsible and Emerson had other risk factors.

feed.title: Exclusive: How John Deere Is Navigating the Ag Downturn, Equipment Costs and Regulatory Crosswinds
feed.summary: Deere & Company President Deanna Kovar discusses how the company is managing current challenges in the agricultural equipment market, including an ag downturn, high equipment costs, and regulatory issues. While forecasting a continued decline in the North American ag equipment market for 2026, Deere is adjusting production, reducing parts prices, and exploring alternative fuel technologies like an E-98 ethanol tractor. The company is also addressing concerns around "Right to Repair" and DEF systems, while emphasizing the importance of technology retrofits and data-driven farming decisions.

feed.title: Jacobs Solutions, Planet Labs, NetApp, TaskUs, and Viasat Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Jacobs Solutions, Planet Labs, NetApp, TaskUs, and Viasat all saw gains after a Consumer Price Index (CPI) report came in lower than anticipated, increasing investor optimism for potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. The softened inflation data, with prices rising 0.2% from December to January against a 0.3% forecast, is leading to increased bets on multiple rate reductions this year, stimulating a market rally. NetApp's shares, in particular, remain somewhat volatile but were positively impacted by the news, though they are still down for the year.

feed.title: Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax, saw significant jumps following a softer-than-anticipated Consumer Price Index (CPI) report. This fueled investor optimism for potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, leading to a rally in affected stocks. The Russell 2000 index, comprising smaller companies, reacted particularly strongly to the positive economic news.

feed.title: HeartBeam (BEAT) Highlighted Among Medical Device Innovators as FDA Clearances Set Tone for New Year
feed.summary: HeartBeam (NASDAQ: BEAT) has been recognized among medical device innovators for its progress in cardiac diagnostics, specifically its HeartBeam System, a cable-free 12-lead ECG platform. The company was highlighted in a Modern Healthcare report for achieving meaningful regulatory milestones, including FDA clearances, and was also featured in PatentVest's "Total Cardiac Intelligence" report. This recognition underscores HeartBeam's forward momentum in advancing its novel approach to assessing arrhythmias.

feed.title: Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax, saw gains after the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) report indicated softer inflation than anticipated. This news fueled investor optimism for potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, leading to a rally in both stocks and Treasuries. The article also provides a detailed look at Gartner's recent stock performance and market reactions to previous news.

feed.title: How to cut data loss risks when employees leave
feed.summary: Employee departures, whether voluntary or involuntary, significantly increase the risk of data loss for organizations. Malicious insider attacks and even accidental data exfiltration can be costly, with financial gain, espionage, and anger often motivating breaches. Implementing a strong Data Loss Prevention (DLP) program, including data classification, continuous monitoring, access controls, and a thorough offboarding process, is crucial to mitigate these risks and protect company assets.

feed.title: Caterpillar group president Kaiser sells $1.3m in stock
feed.summary: Caterpillar Group President Jason Kaiser sold 1,690 shares of company stock for a total of $1.31 million on February 12, 2026. This transaction occurred while the stock was near its 52-week high and showed a 117% return over the past year. InvestingPro analysis suggests Caterpillar is currently overvalued, despite the company reporting strong Q4 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations.

feed.title: Is Viatris (VTRS) Quietly Repositioning Its Deal-Making Strategy With a New Chief Legal Officer?
feed.summary: Viatris (VTRS) recently appointed Matthew J. Maletta as Chief Legal Officer, effective February 9, 2026, signaling a potential reinforcement of its legal capabilities for complex corporate transactions and regulatory matters. Maletta's extensive pharmaceutical legal experience across generics and branded drugs could influence Viatris's investment narrative and risk profile. This strategic hire comes as Viatris continues to focus on broadening its higher-value cardiovascular portfolio and addressing ongoing regulatory issues.

feed.title: Sunoco LP to Announce Q4 Earnings on February 17
feed.summary: Sunoco LP is scheduled to report its Q4 earnings on February 17 before market open, with expected EPS at $0.07 and revenue at $9.36 billion, a significant year-over-year increase. The company has a history of exceeding earnings and revenue estimates, and analysts have recently upgraded its rating to 'Buy' due to its attractive 7% dividend yield. Despite positive performance history, recent estimate revisions indicate cautious market sentiment.

feed.title: Why Is Ingersoll Rand (IR) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand's (IR) stock surged by 6.1% after the company announced better-than-expected fourth-quarter 2025 results, with both adjusted earnings and revenue surpassing analyst forecasts. Despite strong quarterly performance, the company's full-year 2026 guidance for adjusted EBITDA and EPS was slightly below expectations. The market considers this news significant, as IR shares are not typically volatile, and the stock has already reached a new 52-week high, up 25% since the start of the year.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson ordered to pay $250,000 in talc powder lawsuit
feed.summary: A Pennsylvania state court jury has ordered Johnson & Johnson to pay $250,000 to the family of a woman who alleged their talc-based baby powder caused her ovarian cancer. The lawsuit, filed by Gayle Emerson in 2019, claimed the company failed to warn consumers despite knowing the dangers. The jury awarded $50,000 in compensatory damages and $200,000 in punitive damages to her family.

feed.title: Beloved Restaurant Permanently Closing All Locations
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants, Inc., parent company of Bahama Breeze, announced the permanent closure of all Bahama Breeze locations. Half of the 28 remaining restaurants will close their doors completely, while the other 14 will be converted into other Darden brands. This decision follows a previous closure of 15 Bahama Breeze locations in May 2025, with the company stating that the brand was no longer a strategic priority.

feed.title: Vertex’s CRISPR therapy rebounds in latest earnings
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals saw its shares jump after reporting stronger-than-expected sales for its CRISPR gene-edited therapy, Casgevy, tripling sales from the previous quarter. The company reported $54 million in Casgevy sales for Q4 2025, contributing to its overall $12 billion in revenue for the year, and analysts are optimistic about its future sales potential for 2026. Despite challenges like the lengthy treatment process and chemotherapy side effects, patient adoption increased significantly, and Vertex anticipates smoother operations by 2027, further bolstered by potential FDA approval for younger patients and new experimental drugs.

feed.title: Byron Donalds trades in Bitcoin, Howmet Aerospace, and The Trade Desk stocks
feed.summary: Representative Byron Donalds of Florida's 19th congressional district engaged in several stock market transactions recently, as disclosed in a congressional trade report. These transactions included purchasing Bitcoin, and selling portions of his holdings in Howmet Aerospace Inc. and The Trade Desk, Inc. - Class A, all within the range of $1,001 to $15,000 and conducted through his Moran Wealth IRA.

feed.title: T-Mobile US Inc.: How America’s Un?Carrier Turned Its Network Into a Product Powerhouse
feed.summary: T-Mobile US Inc. has transformed its 5G network, plans, and bundled services into a comprehensive product strategy that challenges competitors like AT&T and Verizon. The company's "Un-Carrier" approach focuses on transparency, value-rich subscriptions, and a strong digital ecosystem to drive growth and customer loyalty. This product-centric strategy, combined with technological advantages and a clear brand identity, has positioned T-Mobile as a leading force in the U.S. wireless market, reflected in its stock performance.
